sourceTitle: Everything you can do with Notion AI

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut opens Notion AI on a Windows desktop?

options.text: Ctrl + Shift + N
options.explanation: Ctrl + Shift + N opens a new Notion page, not the AI.

options.text: Shift + Ctrl + J
options.explanation: Shift + Ctrl + J is the documented shortcut for opening Notion AI on Windows.

options.text: Alt + A
options.explanation: Alt + A is not associated with Notion AI.

options.text: Cmd + Shift + J
options.explanation: Cmd is a Mac key; on Windows you need Ctrl, not Cmd.

questions.question: How can you quickly revisit a previous Notion AI conversation?

options.text: Click the 'View History' option in the three‑dot menu of the chat interface.
options.explanation: The article explains that 'View History' in the three‑dot menu shows past conversations.

options.text: Press the backspace key while the chat window is open.
options.explanation: Backspace does not trigger history view.

options.text: Refresh the page and the last conversation will reload automatically.
options.explanation: Refreshing clears the chat; it does not bring back history.

options.text: Search for the phrase ‘Notion AI’ in the global Notion search bar.
options.explanation: Global search finds pages, not specific AI chat logs.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a one‑click skill in Notion AI?

options.text: Translate a page
options.explanation: Translation is explicitly mentioned as a one‑click skill.

options.text: Summarize a PDF
options.explanation: Summarizing PDFs is described under file analysis, not as a one‑click skill.

options.text: Get help with code
options.explanation: Getting coding help is a one‑click skill.

options.text: Create a meeting agenda template
options.explanation: The article does not list ‘Create a meeting agenda template’ as a one‑click skill; it’s covered under template creation later.

questions.question: When you want Notion AI to search only within a specific knowledge hub, which UI element should you use?

options.text: The 'All sources' dropdown at the bottom of the chat box.
options.explanation: The article instructs to select 'All sources' then choose the specific hub.

options.text: The global Notion search bar at the top of the sidebar.
options.explanation: The global search looks across everything, not a limited source.

options.text: The 'View History' button in the chat menu.
options.explanation: View History shows past chats, not source selection.

options.text: The 'Translate' option in the AI menu.
options.explanation: Translate changes language, not search scope.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using an "@" mention when asking Notion AI a question?

options.text: To direct the AI to look at a specific page or document within a source.
options.explanation: The article notes that @ mentions let you target a particular page for more precise answers.

options.text: To tag a teammate so they receive a notification.
options.explanation: While @ can tag teammates in Notion, in the AI context it narrows the source.

options.text: To switch the AI model from GPT‑4 to Claude.
options.explanation: Model selection is not controlled via @ mentions.

options.text: To enable the AI to access external websites.
options.explanation: External web access isn’t granted by @ mentions; it only references internal pages.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes how Notion AI handles data privacy when connecting to external apps like Slack or Google Drive?

options.text: It stores the data in its own training set to improve future AI responses.
options.explanation: The article explicitly says Notion never uses your data for AI training.

options.text: It only shows you information from places you already have access to and does not use it for training.
options.explanation: Notion AI respects existing permissions and does not use the data for training.

options.text: It copies the data to a public Notion workspace for collaboration.
options.explanation: Data is not duplicated to a public workspace.

options.text: It encrypts the data and shares it with third‑party AI providers.
options.explanation: The article does not mention sharing encrypted data with third parties.

questions.question: If you want Notion AI to create a new database for managing social media content, what is the first step you must take?

options.text: Open a new page and click the 'Database' button at the bottom, then select 'Build with AI'.
options.explanation: The guide outlines this exact workflow for building a database with AI.

options.text: Go to Settings → Integrations and enable the 'Database Builder' toggle.
options.explanation: There is no such toggle mentioned.

options.text: Upload a CSV file of your social media posts and let AI import it.
options.explanation: Importing CSV is a manual method, not the described AI flow.

options.text: Ask Notion AI to 'Create a new database' from the chat without opening a page first.
options.explanation: You need to start from a page and click 'Database' before invoking AI.

questions.question: Which feature allows Notion AI to generate visual content based on a text prompt?

options.text: Image creation via a text prompt, followed by iterative refinements.
options.explanation: The article describes creating or editing images by providing a text prompt and refining it.

options.text: PDF summarization with embedded graphics.
options.explanation: PDF summarization deals with text, not image generation.

options.text: One‑click translation of images.
options.explanation: Translation changes language, not creates new visuals.

options.text: Slack connector that pulls image assets from channels.
options.explanation: The Slack connector searches text, not generates images.

questions.question: When using Notion AI to draft a document that must follow a specific style guide, which of the following commands is most appropriate?

options.text: Revise this content to match the style guide @[style guide page].
options.explanation: The article gives this exact phrasing as the way to apply a style guide via @ mention.

options.text: Translate this document using the style guide language.
options.explanation: Translation changes language, not style.

options.text: Summarize the style guide and prepend it to my draft.
options.explanation: Summarizing the guide doesn't enforce its rules on the draft.

options.text: Create a new page titled 'Style Guide Draft'.
options.explanation: Creating a new page doesn't apply the guide to existing content.

questions.question: Which statement accurately reflects the difference between Notion AI’s “All sources” search and selecting a specific source like Google Drive?

options.text: “All sources” searches every connected workspace and app, while a specific source limits the query to that app’s content.
options.explanation: The guide explains that “All sources” is broad, whereas selecting a source narrows the scope.

options.text: Both options search the same data; the dropdown only changes the UI layout.
options.explanation: The dropdown actually changes the search scope.

options.text: Choosing a specific source disables the AI’s ability to use world knowledge.
options.explanation: World knowledge can still be used as fallback if the source lacks an answer.

options.text: “All sources” only includes Notion pages, while specific sources add external apps.
options.explanation: All sources includes both Notion and connected apps, not just Notion pages.
